title: HIT DatabaseSystem - Query Processing & Query Optimisation
summary: Refined implementation-order DAG for the query-processing and query-optimisation lab, split into function-level and algorithm-phase student units.
instructionPreview: This project implements two undergraduate database-lab components: (1) an external-memory relational query processing system and (2) a query-tree optimisation engine. Both are C++ applications built with CMake.
instruction:
This project implements two undergraduate database-lab components: (1) an external-memory relational query processing system and (2) a query-tree optimisation engine. Both are C++ applications built with CMake.

The query processing system operates on two simulated relations R(A,B) (112 tuples, 16 blocks) and S(C,D) (224 tuples, 32 blocks) stored as fixed-size 64-byte block files in `/workspace/lab/QueryProcessing/data/`. A fixed-size buffer (520 bytes, holding up to 8 blocks) provides block-level I/O primitives; all operators must respect this constraint. The compiled binary at `/workspace/lab/QueryProcessing/build/QueryProcessing` accepts interactive commands: `select`, `project`, `nlj`, `hj`, `smj`.

The query optimiser parses textual query representations into tree structures and applies heuristic transformations (predicate pushdown). Its binary is at `/workspace/lab/QueryOptimize/build/QueryOptimize`.

Your working directory is `/workspace`. The project code is already present. You need to implement all the modules and features listed below so that all tests pass.

## Hollowed Units to Implement

- **Buffer Initialization** (`lab/QueryProcessing/extmem.cpp`): implement `initBuffer`, allocating the buffer memory region and setting the core metadata fields.

- **Buffer Block Allocation** (`lab/QueryProcessing/extmem.cpp`): implement `getNewBlockInBuffer`, selecting a free slot and returning its payload pointer.

- **Buffer Block Release** (`lab/QueryProcessing/extmem.cpp`): implement `freeBlockInBuffer`, returning a consumed slot to the free list.

- **Buffer Block Read** (`lab/QueryProcessing/extmem.cpp`): implement `readBlockFromDisk`, loading a numbered block file into an available buffer slot.

- **Buffer Block Write** (`lab/QueryProcessing/extmem.cpp`): implement `writeBlockToDisk`, flushing a buffer slot back to disk and releasing it.

- **Buffered Tuple Writer** (`lab/QueryProcessing/blkio.cpp`): implement `pushToWrite`, batching result tuples into output blocks with reset/push controls.

- **Tuple Block Decoder** (`lab/QueryProcessing/blkio.cpp`): implement `readTuplesFromBlock`, deserialising raw relation blocks into `Relation` tuples.

- **Join Output Emitter** (`lab/QueryProcessing/blkio.cpp`): implement `output`, printing 3-attribute join tuples and forwarding them to disk output.

- **Hash Partition Writer** (`lab/QueryProcessing/blkio.cpp`): implement `pushHashToWrite`, routing tuples into hash buckets on disk.

- **Hash Partition Reader** (`lab/QueryProcessing/blkio.cpp`): implement `readHashFromBlock`, decoding bucket records back into relation tuples.

- **Query Tree Node Output** (`lab/QueryOptimize/tree.cpp`): implement the node-formatting portion of `TreeNode::preOrder` with 4-space indentation.

- **Query Tree Traversal** (`lab/QueryOptimize/tree.cpp`): complete `TreeNode::preOrder` by recursively traversing left and right subtrees.

- **Query Parser** (`lab/QueryOptimize/main.cpp`): implement `parseQuery`, tokenising the relational-algebra string into SELECT/PROJECTION/JOIN/LEAF nodes.

- **Query Optimizer** (`lab/QueryOptimize/main.cpp`): implement `optimizeQuery`, splitting compound predicates and pushing SELECT nodes below JOIN/PROJECTION nodes.

- **External Sort Run Generation** (`lab/QueryProcessing/mergeSort.cpp`): implement pass 1 of `mergeSort`, reading M-block segments, sorting them in memory, and writing sorted runs.

- **External Merge Sort Final Merge** (`lab/QueryProcessing/mergeSort.cpp`): implement pass 2 of `mergeSort`, merging run heads into the final globally sorted output.

- **Selection Scan for R** (`lab/QueryProcessing/select.cpp`): implement the R-side branch of `select`, filtering tuples where `A = 40`.

- **Selection Scan for S and Final Flush** (`lab/QueryProcessing/select.cpp`): complete `select` by filtering S tuples where `C = 60`, then flushing the buffered output.

- **Projection Preparation** (`lab/QueryProcessing/project.cpp`): implement the setup phase of `project`, invoking `mergeSort(buf, SORT_R)` and initialising projection output.

- **Projection Deduplication Scan** (`lab/QueryProcessing/project.cpp`): complete `project` by scanning sorted R output and emitting distinct A values.

- **Hash Join Partitioning Phase** (`lab/QueryProcessing/hashJoin.cpp`): implement phase 1 of `hashJoin`, partitioning both relations into disk buckets.

- **Hash Join Probe Phase** (`lab/QueryProcessing/hashJoin.cpp`): complete `hashJoin` by building/probing buckets and emitting joined tuples.

- **Nested-Loop Outer Block Scan** (`lab/QueryProcessing/nestedLoopJoin.cpp`): implement the outer buffering phase of `nestedLoopJoin`, loading M-1 blocks of R.

- **Nested-Loop Join Inner Probe** (`lab/QueryProcessing/nestedLoopJoin.cpp`): complete `nestedLoopJoin` by scanning S for each buffered R batch and flushing results.

- **Sort-Merge Join Preparation** (`lab/QueryProcessing/sortMergeJoin.cpp`): implement the setup phase of `sortMergeJoin`, sorting both relations and loading the first sorted blocks.

- **Sort-Merge Join Scan** (`lab/QueryProcessing/sortMergeJoin.cpp`): complete `sortMergeJoin` with the two-pointer merge scan that handles duplicate-key groups.

## Test-Coupled Signatures

The following functions are called directly by the test suite through the compiled binaries. You must implement them with these exact signatures:

- `Buffer *initBuffer(size_t bufSize, size_t blkSize, Buffer *buf)` — initialises a buffer with the specified total size and block size, allocating memory and setting metadata fields
- `unsigned char *getNewBlockInBuffer(Buffer *buf)` — allocates and returns a pointer to a free block within the buffer
- `unsigned char *readBlockFromDisk(unsigned int addr, Buffer *buf)` — reads the disk file for the given block address into a buffer slot and returns a pointer to it
- `int writeBlockToDisk(unsigned char *blkPtr, unsigned int addr, Buffer *buf)` — writes a buffer block to disk at the given address
- `void pushToWrite(Buffer *buf, unsigned char *source, size_t size, unsigned int addr, bool reset, bool push)` — buffered tuple writer: accumulates tuples in a block and flushes to disk when full; supports reset and force-push modes
- `void output(Buffer *buf, unsigned int addr, int attr1, int attr2, int attr3)` — outputs a 3-attribute join result tuple to stdout and writes it to disk
- `void pushHashToWrite(Buffer *buf, Relation *R, unsigned int addr, bool reset, bool push)` — hash-partitions a tuple into one of BUCKET_NUM buckets and writes partitions to disk
- `int readHashFromBlock(unsigned char *blk, Relation *R)` — reads hash-partitioned tuples from a block into a Relation array, returns count
- `int select(Buffer *buf)` — performs selection on R and S with target attribute values
- `int project(Buffer *buf)` — performs projection with duplicate elimination on R
- `int mergeSort(Buffer *buf, bool choice)` — two-pass multi-way external merge sort for relation R or S
- `int nestedLoopJoin(Buffer *buf)` — block nested-loop join of R and S on R.A = S.C
- `int hashJoin(Buffer *buf)` — hash join of R and S on R.A = S.C
- `int sortMergeJoin(Buffer *buf)` — sort-merge join of R and S on R.A = S.C
- `void parseQuery(TreeNode *root, string query)` — parses a textual relational-algebra query into a tree of TreeNode objects
- `TreeNode *optimizeQuery(TreeNode *root, vector<string> info)` — applies heuristic optimisation (predicate pushdown) to a query tree
- `void TreeNode::preOrder(int level)` — prints the query tree in pre-order traversal with indentation

## Supporting Helper Signatures

The binaries exercise these helpers transitively, so they must also be implemented correctly:

- `void freeBlockInBuffer(unsigned char *blk, Buffer *buf)` — marks a consumed buffer slot available again
- `void readTuplesFromBlock(unsigned char *blk, Relation *R)` — decodes a block into `TUPLE_PER_BLK` `Relation` tuples

Refer to `extmem.h`, `blkio.h`, `join.h`, `file.h`, and `tree.h` for data structure definitions and constants (`BUF_SIZE`, `BLK_SIZE`, `TUPLE_PER_BLK`, `BUCKET_NUM`, `R_BLK_NUM`, `S_BLK_NUM`, address constants, `Relation`, `Relation3`, `Hash`, `TreeNode`, etc.).

moduleDag.project: HIT DatabaseSystem - Query Processing & Query Optimisation
moduleDag.description: Refined implementation-order DAG for the query-processing and query-optimisation lab, split into function-level and algorithm-phase student units.

nodes.label: Buffer Initialization (initBuffer)
nodes.description: Implements `initBuffer`, allocating the contiguous buffer region and populating the core size/free-block metadata.

nodes.label: Buffer Block Allocation (getNewBlockInBuffer)
nodes.description: Implements `getNewBlockInBuffer`, scanning for an available slot, marking it unavailable, and returning the block payload pointer.

nodes.label: Buffer Block Release (freeBlockInBuffer)
nodes.description: Implements `freeBlockInBuffer`, returning a consumed slot to the free list and restoring the free-block counter.

nodes.label: Buffer Block Read (readBlockFromDisk)
nodes.description: Implements `readBlockFromDisk`, loading a numbered `.blk` file into an available buffer slot and tracking I/O usage.

nodes.label: Buffer Block Write (writeBlockToDisk)
nodes.description: Implements `writeBlockToDisk`, flushing a buffer slot back to disk, releasing the slot, and incrementing I/O counters.

nodes.label: Buffered Tuple Writer (pushToWrite)
nodes.description: Implements `pushToWrite`, the reusable buffered writer that batches tuple payloads into result blocks and flushes them on demand.

nodes.label: Tuple Block Decoder (readTuplesFromBlock)
nodes.description: Implements `readTuplesFromBlock`, deserialising fixed-width relation tuples from a raw disk block into a `Relation` array.

nodes.label: Join Output Emitter (output)
nodes.description: Implements `output`, printing a three-attribute join tuple and forwarding it through the buffered writer.

nodes.label: Hash Partition Writer (pushHashToWrite)
nodes.description: Implements `pushHashToWrite`, hashing tuples into `BUCKET_NUM` disk partitions with per-bucket buffered blocks.

nodes.label: Hash Partition Reader (readHashFromBlock)
nodes.description: Implements `readHashFromBlock`, decoding hash-bucket records back into `Relation` tuples for the hash join probe phase.

nodes.label: Query Tree Node Output
nodes.description: Implements the node-formatting half of `TreeNode::preOrder`, printing LEAF versus operator nodes with 4-space indentation.

nodes.label: Query Tree Traversal
nodes.description: Completes `TreeNode::preOrder` by recursively traversing left and right children after printing the current node.

nodes.label: Query Parser (parseQuery)
nodes.description: Implements `parseQuery`, tokenising the relational-algebra string and building the SELECT/PROJECTION/JOIN/LEAF tree structure.

nodes.label: Query Optimizer (optimizeQuery)
nodes.description: Implements `optimizeQuery`, splitting compound predicates and pushing SELECT nodes below JOIN/PROJECTION nodes.

nodes.label: External Sort Run Generation
nodes.description: Implements the first pass of `mergeSort`, reading M-block segments, sorting them in memory, and writing sorted runs to disk.

nodes.label: External Merge Sort Final Merge
nodes.description: Completes `mergeSort` with the second-pass multi-way merge that scans run heads and emits globally sorted output blocks.

nodes.label: Selection Scan for Relation R
nodes.description: Implements the first half of `select`, scanning relation R for tuples where `A = 40` and feeding matches into the shared writer.

nodes.label: Selection Scan for Relation S and Final Flush
nodes.description: Completes `select` by scanning relation S for `C = 60`, flushing remaining output blocks, and reporting the final I/O count.

nodes.label: Projection Preparation
nodes.description: Implements the setup phase of `project`, invoking `mergeSort(buf, SORT_R)` and initialising the projection result writer.

nodes.label: Projection Deduplication Scan
nodes.description: Completes `project` with the single-pass scan over sorted R output that emits each distinct attribute A exactly once.

nodes.label: Hash Join Partitioning Phase
nodes.description: Implements phase 1 of `hashJoin`, partitioning both R and S into hash buckets on disk using the block I/O helpers.

nodes.label: Hash Join Probe Phase
nodes.description: Completes `hashJoin` by loading bucketed R partitions, probing with S partitions, and emitting joined tuples.

nodes.label: Nested-Loop Outer Block Scan
nodes.description: Implements the outer-block buffering phase of `nestedLoopJoin`, loading M-1 blocks of relation R at a time.

nodes.label: Nested-Loop Join Inner Probe
nodes.description: Completes `nestedLoopJoin` by scanning S for each buffered R batch, emitting matches, and flushing the final output.

nodes.label: Sort-Merge Join Preparation
nodes.description: Implements the setup phase of `sortMergeJoin`, sorting both relations, resetting the result writer, and loading the first sorted blocks.

nodes.label: Sort-Merge Join Scan
nodes.description: Completes `sortMergeJoin` with the two-pointer merge scan that groups equal keys and emits all join combinations.
